fields.Text:
10-month-old with seizures and intracranial calcifications. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Congenital CMV infection}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q1. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Congenital CMV infection shows characteristic periventricular calcifications on CT, which are pathognomonic. Additional findings include ventriculomegaly, reduced cortical sulcation with simplified gyral pattern, and cerebellar hypoplasia.

fields.Text:
High-speed MVC with focal outpouching at the aortic isthmus. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Traumatic aortic pseudoaneurysm}}
fields.Extra: Q2. Correct; difficulty: hard. Sagittal contrast-enhanced CT shows an irregular and somewhat angulated outpouching at the medial aspect of the aortic isthmus, consistent with pseudoaneurysm.

fields.Text:
Lateral talar/calcaneal marrow edema at the angle of Gissane. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Lateral hindfoot impingement}}
fields.Extra: Q4. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: T1 hypointense signal in lateral talus and calcaneus represents talocalcaneal impingement from adult acquired flatfoot deformity, where hindfoot valgus causes abnormal bone contact and marrow edema.

fields.Text:
40-year-old with right pelvic pain and avascular cystic ovarian lesion with reticular internal echoes. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Hemorrhagic ovarian cyst}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q6. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Avascular cystic structure with diffuse reticular internal echoes represents hemorrhagic cyst. The lacy, net-like pattern from fibrin strands is characteristic. Typically resolves on follow-up, distinguishing it from persistent endometriomas with ground-glass appearance.

fields.Text:
Shoulder injury with Hill-Sachs and Bankart lesions. Mechanism?

{{c1::Anterior shoulder dislocation}}
fields.Extra: Q7. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. The frontal shoulder radiograph shows deformity at the posterior lateral aspect of the humeral head (green arrows) consistent with a Hill-Sachs impaction fracture.

fields.Text:
Marked endometrial vascularity with high diastolic flow after D&C. Mechanism?

{{c1::Low-resistance vascular bed}}
fields.Extra: Q8. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Low resistance vascular beds show low pulsatility arterial waveforms with continuous diastolic flow. Spectral Doppler demonstrates high peak systolic velocities with maintained diastolic flow and spectral broadening. Color Doppler displays prominent vessels that "light up" with high velocity flow throughout the cardiac cycle.

fields.Text:
Cardiac image with arrowed RV trabecular structure. What structure is indicated?

{{c1::Moderator band}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q10. Correct; difficulty: easy. The moderator band is one of the trabeculae carneae in the right ventricle.

fields.Text:
Breast mammogram with lucent fat-density center and eggshell rim calcification. Most likely association?

{{c1::Prior trauma or surgery}}
fields.Extra: Q11. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: "Oil cysts" with fat necrosis are thin capsules around a fat density center creating an "eggshell" appearance. Fat necrosis can occur with prior trauma (or prior surgery).

fields.Text:
Well-defined hyperechoic hepatic mass with posterior acoustic enhancement. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Hepatic hemangioma}}
fields.Extra: Q12. Correct; difficulty: easy. Hemangiomas are composed of variably sized vascular spaces lined by flat endothelial cells with surrounding fibrous stroma, which increase as the lesion ages.

fields.Text:
18-year-old with heel pain and expansile calcaneal lesion with fluid-fluid levels. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Aneurysmal bone cyst}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q13. Correct; difficulty: hard. The sagittal fat-saturated T2-weighted MR shows an expansile lesion in the posterior calcaneus with numerous cystic spaces and fluid-fluid levels (blue arrows) as well as surrounding marrow edema (red arrow).

fields.Text:
Gunshot wounds with pneumomediastinum and contrast extravasation from the upper thoracic esophagus. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Esophageal perforation}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q14. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The CT shows pneumomediastinum, left small pneumothorax, and left apical pulmonary contusion.

fields.Text:
Dumbbell-shaped left intercostal/chest wall mass with extrapleural extension. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Desmoid tumor}}
fields.Extra: Q15. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Coronal T1 pre-contrast (left), axial T1 fat saturation pre- and post-contrast (middle) and T2 fat saturation (right) magnetic resonance images of the left chest wall show a dumbbell-shaped mass centered in the left intercostal space with extrapleural extension into the left thorax (blue arrows).

fields.Text:
Solid-food dysphagia with posterior esophageal impression from aberrant vessel. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Left aortic arch with aberrant right subclavian artery}}
fields.Extra: Q17. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Left aortic arch with aberrant right subclavian artery presenting with dysphagia represents "dysphagia lusoria" caused by the aberrant vessel coursing posterior to the esophagus. As the aberrant right subclavian artery travels from its anomalous origin as the last branch of the aortic arch to reach the right arm, it passes behind and compresses the esophagus, causing extrinsic compression and swallowing difficulty. This is the classic presentation of the most common congenital arch anomaly when it becomes symptomatic.

fields.Text:
Older woman with nonproductive cough and right middle lobe/lingular bronchiectasis. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Nontuberculous mycobacterial infection}}
fields.Extra: Q18. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Mycobacterium avium-intracellulare should be considered in older female patients with right middle lobe and lingular bronchiectasis.

fields.Text:
Upper extremity angiogram with early venous filling during arterial phase. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Arteriovenous fistula}}
fields.Extra: Q19. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Post-trauma arteriovenous fistula in the upper extremity appears on DSA as early venous opacification during the arterial phase, with direct communication between an artery and vein. DSA precisely identifies the feeding artery, draining vein, and exact site of abnormal communication, and may reveal associated complications like pseudoaneurysms from repeated vascular injury.

fields.Text:
Post-renal-transplant MAG3 scan with tracer in pelvis/surgical drain. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Anastomotic leak}}
fields.Extra: Q20. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. This is a Tc99m-MAG3 renal scan.

fields.Text:
Uterine US with echogenic mass containing fat/muscle components. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Lipoleiomyoma}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q21. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Lipoleiomyomas appear hyperechoic due to acoustic impedance mismatch between fat and muscle, creating strong reflective interfaces. Internal septations from mixed fat/muscle composition and minimal vascularity of mature fat tissue complete the characteristic appearance.

fields.Text:
Stereotactic biopsy post-fire diagram. What targeting error is present?

{{c1::Y-axis error}}
fields.Extra: Q22. Correct; difficulty: hard. Pre-fire and post-fire -15 degrees (left) and +15 degrees (right) images are acquired during stereotactic biopsy.

fields.Text:
Breast MRI with spiculated margins, rim enhancement, and washout kinetics. BI-RADS category?

{{c1::BI-RADS 5}}
fields.Extra: Q23. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Spiculated margins, rim enhancement, and washout kinetics on breast MRI constitute BI-RADS 5 assessment with greater than 95% cancer probability, mandating immediate biopsy and surgical consultation.

fields.Text:
Uterine anomaly MRI: best view to distinguish septate from bicornuate uterus?

{{c1::Coronal view showing both uterine ostia at fundal level}}
fields.Extra: Q25. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Explanation: Tissue dividing the endometrial cavity extends from the fundus to the external cervical os and may extend into the vagina.

fields.Text:
Post-ACL reconstruction with rounded fibrous mass in the anterior intercondylar notch. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Arthrofibrosis/cyclops lesion}}
fields.Extra: Q26. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: A cyclops lesion (localized anterior arthrofibrosis) is a common complication of reconstruction of the anterior cruciate ligament caused by excessive scar tissue formation within the joint capsule. It consists of a rounded fibrous mass in the anterior intercondylar notch that is formed of central granulation tissued lined by synovium and surrounded by dense fibrous tissue. This entity can also be iatrogenic/post-traumatic, as seen in this case.

fields.Text:
Throat pain/dysphagia from elongated styloid process. Abnormal length threshold?

{{c1::Greater than 3 cm}}

1/8
fields.Extra: Q27. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Eagle syndrome causes throat pain and dysphagia from elongated styloid process (>3 cm vs normal approximately 2.5 cm). Only 4% to 10% with elongated styloid are symptomatic.

fields.Text:
Postmenopausal uterine US with endometrial thickness shown. Normal upper limit?

{{c1::5 mm}}
fields.Extra: Q29. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. The sagittal uterine ultrasound demonstrates a slightly heterogeneous-appearing endometrium with a thickness of 1.8cm (red arrow), which is abnormally thickened.

fields.Text:
Assisted reproduction with enlarged multicystic ovaries and ascites. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Ovarian hyperstimulation syndrome}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q30. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Multiple bilateral prominent ovarian cysts with ascites indicate ovarian hyperstimulation syndrome from assisted reproductive technology. Presence of ascites defines moderate-grade OHSS requiring careful monitoring for progression to severe disease with potential life-threatening complications including thromboembolic events.

fields.Text:
Ascites with small bowel serosal nodularity and peritoneal thickening/nodularity. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Peritoneal carcinomatosis}}
fields.Extra: Q31. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The combination of small bowel serosal nodularity (blue arrows) and large volume ascites (yellow arrows) and peritoneal thickening and nodularity (green arrows) should raise suspicion for peritoneal metastasis with serosal involvement and associated malignant ascites, especially in the setting of a history of breast ca

fields.Text:
Thyroid scan with nonfunctioning/cold nodule. Next step?

{{c1::Thyroid ultrasound correlation}}
fields.Extra: Q32. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Though cold nodules tend to have a greater malignancy risk than hot nodules, most are still benign. Cold appearance alone is insufficient for definitive cancer diagnosis; correlation with ultrasound features and clinical context is essential.

fields.Text:
Thumb UCL avulsion with displaced ligament superficial to aponeurosis. What structure causes the Stener lesion?

{{c1::Adductor pollicis aponeurosis}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q33. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The coronal images through the thumb show complete avulsion of the ulnar collateral ligament (UCL – pink arrows) off the base of the first proximal phalanx (blue arrows).

fields.Text:
Nuclear medicine device shown for measuring activity in vials/syringes. Device?

{{c1::Dose calibrator}}
fields.Extra: Q34. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Dose calibrators are sealed ionization chambers used to measure radioactivity in vials/syringes for patient dosing, handling higher activities. Well counters are scintillation detectors with cylindrical crystal wells for high-sensitivity counting of low-level specimens (blood, urine, wipes), limited to activities up to 0.001 mCi due to dead-time losses at higher activities.

fields.Text:
Prenatal US with absent fetal kidneys/oligohydramnios. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Renal agenesis}}
fields.Extra: Q35. Correct; difficulty: easy. Without kidneys, urine cannot be formed which is required to form amniotic fluid.

fields.Text:
Pediatric sellar/suprasellar mass with calcification and cystic components. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Craniopharyngioma}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q36. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Pediatric craniopharyngiomas are exclusively adamantinomatous type with uniform imaging features. They consistently demonstrate calcification on CT and T1 bright signal intensity on MRI. Cystic components are nearly universal. These features are much more consistent in children compared to adults, where imaging findings are more variable.

fields.Text:
Pedunculated bony excrescence with cortical/medullary continuity and cartilage cap. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Osteochondroma}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q37. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The axial fat-saturated T2 and proton density MR images show a pedunculated bony excrescence with both cortical (red arrow) and medullary (yellow arrow) components, as well as a hyperintense cartilage cap (orange arrows).

fields.Text:
Finger volar tendon sheath mass with T1 iso/hypointensity and homogeneous enhancement. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Giant cell tumor of tendon sheath}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q38. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: T1 iso/hypointense ovoid mass involving volar tendon sheath with homogeneous enhancement suggests localized tenosynovial giant cell tumor, typically presenting as painless soft tissue mass in fingers.

fields.Text:
Cirrhotic liver with reticular T2 hyperintense septa/bridges. What does this represent?

{{c1::Fibrosis}}
fields.Extra: Q39. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Reticular T2 hyperintense signal in cirrhotic liver represents fibrotic septa and bridges with high water content that surround regenerative nodules - this net-like pattern of T2 hyperintense fibrosis is a key imaging feature of advanced cirrhosis.

fields.Text:
Postpartum/procedural uterine Doppler with early venous filling. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Uterine arteriovenous fistula}}
fields.Extra: Q41. Correct; difficulty: easy. Arteriovenous fistulae can occur in the setting of C-section, D&C, multiple pregnancies or can be congenital.

fields.Text:
Post-lateral patellar dislocation surgery with tibial tuberosity transfer and MPFL reconstruction. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Expected post-treatment appearance}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q42. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The axial proton density images show a screw in the medial patellar facet (yellow arrow), which is attaching a thickened and scar-remolded medial patellofemoral ligament (MPFL, red arrow).

fields.Text:
Complex renal cystic mass with rim enhancement and necrotic material. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Intrarenal abscess}}
fields.Extra: Q43. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Renal abscess appears as a sharply marginated complex cystic mass with peripheral rim enhancement and internal necrotic material. Early abscesses show ill-defined areas of decreased attenuation. Associated findings include perilesional edema, perinephric fat stranding, and potential extension beyond the renal parenchyma.

fields.Text:
Post-EVAR aneurysm sac enhancement from retrograde branch-vessel flow. Endoleak type?

{{c1::Type 2 endoleak}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q44. Incorrect; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Type 2 endoleaks result from retrograde collateral flow into the aneurysm sac. Patent branch vessels (primarily IMA and lumbar arteries) remain unsealed after EVAR and can persistently flow into the excluded aneurysm sac.

fields.Text:
MRI with low liver signal relative to spleen on in-phase imaging. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Primary hemochromatosis}}
fields.Extra: Q45. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: MRI in the setting of primary hemochromatosis shows markedly decreased signal intensity of the liver compared with the spleen on in-phase imaging. Thalassemia, sickle cell anemia, frequent transfusions and other diseases can lead to hemosiderosis and secondary hemochromatosis which would be expected to involve the liver, bone marrow, and spleen.

fields.Text:
Cortically based lucent metaphyseal lesion with thin sclerotic rim. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Non-ossifying fibroma}}
fields.Extra: Q46. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. The imaging characteristics of this lesion include a cortically-based lucent centered lesion with a thin, sclerotic rim.

fields.Text:
Wrist radiograph with lunate-triquetrum fusion. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Lunotriquetral coalition}}
fields.Extra: Q47. Correct; difficulty: easy. There is abnormal fusion of the lunate and triquetrum in this case.

fields.Text:
Cardiac MRI/echo with deep intertrabecular recesses and prominent trabeculation. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Left ventricular non-compaction}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q48. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. This is an abnormal arrest in the process of myocardial compaction leading to the development of deep intertrabecular recesses and prominent ventricular trabeculation.

fields.Text:
Neck nodal diagram: node anterior to posterior edge of SCM and posterior to submandibular gland. Level?

{{c1::Level IIA}}
fields.Extra: Q49. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Level II extends from the skull base, at the lower level of the bony margin of the jugular fossa, to the level of the lower body of the hyoid bone.

fields.Text:
Radiography technique change that increases subject contrast. What change?

{{c1::Lower kVp}}
fields.Extra: Q50. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Lowering kVp improves subject contrast as differential attenuation between tissues is greater at lower energies.

fields.Text: Congenital CMV infection classically causes {{c1::periventricular calcifications}} on CT.
fields.Extra: Q1. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Congenital CMV infection shows characteristic periventricular calcifications on CT, which are pathognomonic. Additional findings include ventriculomegaly, reduced cortical sulcation with simplified gyral pattern, and cerebellar hypoplasia.

fields.Text: Traumatic aortic pseudoaneurysm after high-speed deceleration injury most commonly occurs at the {{c1::aortic isthmus}}.
fields.Extra: Q2. Correct; difficulty: hard. Sagittal contrast-enhanced CT shows an irregular and somewhat angulated outpouching at the medial aspect of the aortic isthmus, consistent with pseudoaneurysm.

fields.Text: Using an antiscatter grid in mammography improves image quality but requires a {{c1::higher radiation dose}}.
fields.Extra: Q3. Correct; difficulty: easy. Dosimetry in mammography is an important topic that will appear on the CORE.

fields.Text: Lateral hindfoot impingement most commonly occurs from {{c1::adult acquired flatfoot deformity}} with chronic hindfoot valgus.
fields.Extra: Q4. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: T1 hypointense signal in lateral talus and calcaneus represents talocalcaneal impingement from adult acquired flatfoot deformity, where hindfoot valgus causes abnormal bone contact and marrow edema.

fields.Text: A just culture distinguishes unsafe events as {{c1::human error, at-risk behavior, or reckless behavior}}.
fields.Extra: Q5. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Just culture model distinguishes errors as human error, at-risk behavior, or reckless behavior.

fields.Text: An avascular ovarian cyst with diffuse reticular/lacy internal echoes is a {{c1::hemorrhagic cyst}}.
fields.Extra: Q6. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Avascular cystic structure with diffuse reticular internal echoes represents hemorrhagic cyst. The lacy, net-like pattern from fibrin strands is characteristic. Typically resolves on follow-up, distinguishing it from persistent endometriomas with ground-glass appearance.

fields.Text: Hill-Sachs and Bankart lesions are associated with {{c1::anterior shoulder dislocation}}.
fields.Extra: Q7. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. The frontal shoulder radiograph shows deformity at the posterior lateral aspect of the humeral head (green arrows) consistent with a Hill-Sachs impaction fracture.

fields.Text: Retained products/placental vascular beds show {{c1::low-resistance Doppler waveforms with continuous diastolic flow}}.
fields.Extra: Q8. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Low resistance vascular beds show low pulsatility arterial waveforms with continuous diastolic flow. Spectral Doppler demonstrates high peak systolic velocities with maintained diastolic flow and spectral broadening. Color Doppler displays prominent vessels that "light up" with high velocity flow throughout the cardiac cycle.

fields.Text: Policies that prioritize throughput over patient safety violate {{c1::primacy of patient welfare}}.
fields.Extra: Q9. Correct; difficulty: easy. The administration's policy is jeopardizing patient safety in an attempt to increase efficiency.

fields.Text: The moderator band helps identify the {{c1::anatomic right ventricle}}.
fields.Extra: Q10. Correct; difficulty: easy. The moderator band is one of the trabeculae carneae in the right ventricle.

fields.Text: Breast oil cyst/fat necrosis with eggshell calcification is commonly associated with {{c1::prior trauma or surgery}}.
fields.Extra: Q11. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: "Oil cysts" with fat necrosis are thin capsules around a fat density center creating an "eggshell" appearance. Fat necrosis can occur with prior trauma (or prior surgery).

fields.Text: Hepatic hemangiomas are histologically composed of {{c1::thin-walled endothelial-lined vascular channels}}.
fields.Extra: Q12. Correct; difficulty: easy. Hemangiomas are composed of variably sized vascular spaces lined by flat endothelial cells with surrounding fibrous stroma, which increase as the lesion ages.

fields.Text: An aneurysmal bone cyst can show the {{c1::doughnut sign}} on bone scintigraphy.
fields.Extra: Q13. Correct; difficulty: hard. The sagittal fat-saturated T2-weighted MR shows an expansile lesion in the posterior calcaneus with numerous cystic spaces and fluid-fluid levels (blue arrows) as well as surrounding marrow edema (red arrow).

fields.Text: Suspected esophageal perforation should be evaluated with {{c1::water-soluble contrast}} to avoid mediastinitis from barium leakage.
fields.Extra: Q14. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The CT shows pneumomediastinum, left small pneumothorax, and left apical pulmonary contusion.

fields.Text: Desmoid tumors have {{c1::high recurrence rates after surgical resection}}.
fields.Extra: Q15. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Coronal T1 pre-contrast (left), axial T1 fat saturation pre- and post-contrast (middle) and T2 fat saturation (right) magnetic resonance images of the left chest wall show a dumbbell-shaped mass centered in the left intercostal space with extrapleural extension into the left thorax (blue arrows).

fields.Text: Dose calibrator accuracy testing is performed {{c1::annually}}.
fields.Extra: Q16. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Dose calibrator QC includes four essential tests with specific frequencies: daily constancy testing ensures day-to-day reliability, quarterly linearity testing verifies accurate response across all activity ranges, annual accuracy testing confirms measurement precision against traceable standards, and geometry testing at installation/repair ensures consistent readings across different vial and syringe sizes. This tiered approach balances patient safety with practical workflow demands.

fields.Text: Dysphagia lusoria is caused by an aberrant right subclavian artery passing {{c1::posterior to the esophagus}}.
fields.Extra: Q17. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Left aortic arch with aberrant right subclavian artery presenting with dysphagia represents "dysphagia lusoria" caused by the aberrant vessel coursing posterior to the esophagus. As the aberrant right subclavian artery travels from its anomalous origin as the last branch of the aortic arch to reach the right arm, it passes behind and compresses the esophagus, causing extrinsic compression and swallowing difficulty. This is the classic presentation of the most common congenital arch anomaly when it becomes symptomatic.

fields.Text: Older women with right middle lobe and lingular bronchiectasis should raise concern for {{c1::Mycobacterium avium-intracellulare/nontuberculous mycobacterial infection}}.
fields.Extra: Q18. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Mycobacterium avium-intracellulare should be considered in older female patients with right middle lobe and lingular bronchiectasis.

fields.Text: An arteriovenous fistula on angiography shows {{c1::early venous opacification during the arterial phase}}.
fields.Extra: Q19. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Post-trauma arteriovenous fistula in the upper extremity appears on DSA as early venous opacification during the arterial phase, with direct communication between an artery and vein. DSA precisely identifies the feeding artery, draining vein, and exact site of abnormal communication, and may reveal associated complications like pseudoaneurysms from repeated vascular injury.

fields.Text: Tracer activity in a surgical drain after renal transplant on MAG3 renography suggests {{c1::urinary/anastomotic leak}}.
fields.Extra: Q20. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. This is a Tc99m-MAG3 renal scan.

fields.Text: Uterine lipoleiomyomas appear hyperechoic because of {{c1::fat mixed with smooth muscle/fibrous tissue}}.
fields.Extra: Q21. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Lipoleiomyomas appear hyperechoic due to acoustic impedance mismatch between fat and muscle, creating strong reflective interfaces. Internal septations from mixed fat/muscle composition and minimal vascularity of mature fat tissue complete the characteristic appearance.

fields.Text: In stereotactic biopsy targeting, symmetric post-fire needle position on +/-15 degree images with depth mismatch indicates {{c1::y-axis error}}.
fields.Extra: Q22. Correct; difficulty: hard. Pre-fire and post-fire -15 degrees (left) and +15 degrees (right) images are acquired during stereotactic biopsy.

fields.Text: Spiculated margins, rim enhancement, and washout kinetics on breast MRI are {{c1::BI-RADS 5}}.
fields.Extra: Q23. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Spiculated margins, rim enhancement, and washout kinetics on breast MRI constitute BI-RADS 5 assessment with greater than 95% cancer probability, mandating immediate biopsy and surgical consultation.

fields.Text: Radiation-induced cancer is a {{c1::stochastic}} effect.
fields.Extra: Q24. Correct; difficulty: easy. In radiation biology, there are two main types of effects induced by radiation exposure: stochastic and deterministic.

fields.Text: The key MRI plane for distinguishing septate from bicornuate uterus is a {{c1::coronal view showing both uterine ostia at the fundus}}.
fields.Extra: Q25. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Explanation: Tissue dividing the endometrial cavity extends from the fundus to the external cervical os and may extend into the vagina.

fields.Text: A cyclops lesion is localized anterior {{c1::arthrofibrosis}} after ACL reconstruction.
fields.Extra: Q26. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: A cyclops lesion (localized anterior arthrofibrosis) is a common complication of reconstruction of the anterior cruciate ligament caused by excessive scar tissue formation within the joint capsule. It consists of a rounded fibrous mass in the anterior intercondylar notch that is formed of central granulation tissued lined by synovium and surrounded by dense fibrous tissue. This entity can also be iatrogenic/post-traumatic, as seen in this case.

fields.Text: An elongated styloid process is usually defined as longer than {{c1::3 cm}}.
fields.Extra: Q27. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Eagle syndrome causes throat pain and dysphagia from elongated styloid process (>3 cm vs normal approximately 2.5 cm). Only 4% to 10% with elongated styloid are symptomatic.

fields.Text: HASTE collects {{c1::more data points per TR interval}} than conventional spin-echo imaging.
fields.Extra: Q28. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Half-Fourier-Acquired Single-shot Turbo spin Echo (HASTE) is a Turbo spin-echo technique that is used for sequential acquisition of high-resolution T2-weighted images.

fields.Text: Postmenopausal endometrial thickness greater than {{c1::5 mm}} is abnormal.
fields.Extra: Q29. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. The sagittal uterine ultrasound demonstrates a slightly heterogeneous-appearing endometrium with a thickness of 1.8cm (red arrow), which is abnormally thickened.

fields.Text: Ovarian hyperstimulation syndrome is suggested by {{c1::bilateral enlarged multicystic ovaries with ascites}} after assisted reproduction.
fields.Extra: Q30. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Multiple bilateral prominent ovarian cysts with ascites indicate ovarian hyperstimulation syndrome from assisted reproductive technology. Presence of ascites defines moderate-grade OHSS requiring careful monitoring for progression to severe disease with potential life-threatening complications including thromboembolic events.

fields.Text: Small bowel serosal nodularity with ascites and peritoneal thickening/nodularity suggests {{c1::peritoneal carcinomatosis}}.
fields.Extra: Q31. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The combination of small bowel serosal nodularity (blue arrows) and large volume ascites (yellow arrows) and peritoneal thickening and nodularity (green arrows) should raise suspicion for peritoneal metastasis with serosal involvement and associated malignant ascites, especially in the setting of a history of breast ca

fields.Text: A cold thyroid nodule on scintigraphy requires {{c1::ultrasound correlation}} because most cold nodules are still benign.
fields.Extra: Q32. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Though cold nodules tend to have a greater malignancy risk than hot nodules, most are still benign. Cold appearance alone is insufficient for definitive cancer diagnosis; correlation with ultrasound features and clinical context is essential.

fields.Text: A Stener lesion occurs when the torn thumb UCL is displaced superficial to the {{c1::adductor pollicis aponeurosis}}.
fields.Extra: Q33. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The coronal images through the thumb show complete avulsion of the ulnar collateral ligament (UCL – pink arrows) off the base of the first proximal phalanx (blue arrows).

fields.Text: A dose calibrator is a sealed {{c1::ionization chamber}} used to measure radioactivity in vials/syringes for patient dosing.
fields.Extra: Q34. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Dose calibrators are sealed ionization chambers used to measure radioactivity in vials/syringes for patient dosing, handling higher activities. Well counters are scintillation detectors with cylindrical crystal wells for high-sensitivity counting of low-level specimens (blood, urine, wipes), limited to activities up to 0.001 mCi due to dead-time losses at higher activities.

fields.Text: Bilateral renal agenesis causes severe oligohydramnios because fetal urine is required to form {{c1::amniotic fluid}}.
fields.Extra: Q35. Correct; difficulty: easy. Without kidneys, urine cannot be formed which is required to form amniotic fluid.

fields.Text: Pediatric craniopharyngiomas are adamantinomatous and classically show {{c1::calcification and cystic components}}.
fields.Extra: Q36. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Pediatric craniopharyngiomas are exclusively adamantinomatous type with uniform imaging features. They consistently demonstrate calcification on CT and T1 bright signal intensity on MRI. Cystic components are nearly universal. These features are much more consistent in children compared to adults, where imaging findings are more variable.

fields.Text: Osteochondromas are {{c1::usually low-grade/benign cartilage-capped bony neoplasms}}.
fields.Extra: Q37. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The axial fat-saturated T2 and proton density MR images show a pedunculated bony excrescence with both cortical (red arrow) and medullary (yellow arrow) components, as well as a hyperintense cartilage cap (orange arrows).

fields.Text: Localized tenosynovial giant cell tumor typically presents as a {{c1::painless soft tissue mass involving the finger tendon sheath}}.
fields.Extra: Q38. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: T1 iso/hypointense ovoid mass involving volar tendon sheath with homogeneous enhancement suggests localized tenosynovial giant cell tumor, typically presenting as painless soft tissue mass in fingers.

fields.Text: Reticular T2 hyperintense septa/bridges in cirrhosis represent {{c1::fibrosis}}.
fields.Extra: Q39. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Reticular T2 hyperintense signal in cirrhotic liver represents fibrotic septa and bridges with high water content that surround regenerative nodules - this net-like pattern of T2 hyperintense fibrosis is a key imaging feature of advanced cirrhosis.

fields.Text: MQSA requires written negative mammogram results to the referring provider within {{c1::30 days}}.
fields.Extra: Q40. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: According to the Mammography Quality Standards Act (MQSA)mammography facilities are required to provide patient's referring healthcare provider with written results of a negative mammogram within 30 days. If the mammogram findings are "suspicious" or 'highly suggestive of malignancy", the MQSA requires mammography facilities to provide patient's referring healthcare provider with written results within 7 days.

fields.Text: Uterine AV fistula can occur after {{c1::C-section, D&C, multiple pregnancies, or congenitally}}.
fields.Extra: Q41. Correct; difficulty: easy. Arteriovenous fistulae can occur in the setting of C-section, D&C, multiple pregnancies or can be congenital.

fields.Text: Lateral patellar dislocation can be treated with {{c1::tibial tuberosity transfer and MPFL reconstruction}}.
fields.Extra: Q42. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The axial proton density images show a screw in the medial patellar facet (yellow arrow), which is attaching a thickened and scar-remolded medial patellofemoral ligament (MPFL, red arrow).

fields.Text: Renal abscess appears as a {{c1::rim-enhancing complex cystic mass with internal necrotic material}}.
fields.Extra: Q43. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Renal abscess appears as a sharply marginated complex cystic mass with peripheral rim enhancement and internal necrotic material. Early abscesses show ill-defined areas of decreased attenuation. Associated findings include perilesional edema, perinephric fat stranding, and potential extension beyond the renal parenchyma.

fields.Text: Type 2 endoleak is caused by {{c1::retrograde collateral flow from patent branch vessels into the aneurysm sac}}.
fields.Extra: Q44. Incorrect; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Type 2 endoleaks result from retrograde collateral flow into the aneurysm sac. Patent branch vessels (primarily IMA and lumbar arteries) remain unsealed after EVAR and can persistently flow into the excluded aneurysm sac.

fields.Text: Primary hemochromatosis causes markedly decreased MRI signal in the {{c1::liver relative to the spleen}}.
fields.Extra: Q45. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: MRI in the setting of primary hemochromatosis shows markedly decreased signal intensity of the liver compared with the spleen on in-phase imaging. Thalassemia, sickle cell anemia, frequent transfusions and other diseases can lead to hemosiderosis and secondary hemochromatosis which would be expected to involve the liver, bone marrow, and spleen.

fields.Text: A non-ossifying fibroma is a {{c1::cortically based lucent lesion with a thin sclerotic rim}}.
fields.Extra: Q46. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. The imaging characteristics of this lesion include a cortically-based lucent centered lesion with a thin, sclerotic rim.

fields.Text: Lunotriquetral coalition is fusion of the {{c1::lunate and triquetrum}}.
fields.Extra: Q47. Correct; difficulty: easy. There is abnormal fusion of the lunate and triquetrum in this case.

fields.Text: Left ventricular non-compaction results from arrested myocardial compaction with {{c1::deep intertrabecular recesses and prominent trabeculations}}.
fields.Extra: Q48. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. This is an abnormal arrest in the process of myocardial compaction leading to the development of deep intertrabecular recesses and prominent ventricular trabeculation.

fields.Text: Level IIA nodes are {{c1::anterior to the posterior edge of the SCM}} and posterior to the submandibular gland.
fields.Extra: Q49. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Level II extends from the skull base, at the lower level of the bony margin of the jugular fossa, to the level of the lower body of the hyoid bone.

fields.Text: Lowering kVp increases {{c1::subject contrast}} because tissue attenuation differs more at lower photon energies.
fields.Extra: Q50. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Lowering kVp improves subject contrast as differential attenuation between tissues is greater at lower energies.

fields.Text: Congenital toxoplasmosis can mimic CMV, but congenital CMV is the classic cause of {{c1::periventricular calcifications}} and is the most common intrauterine infection.
fields.Extra: Q1. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Congenital CMV infection shows characteristic periventricular calcifications on CT, which are pathognomonic. Additional findings include ventriculomegaly, reduced cortical sulcation with simplified gyral pattern, and cerebellar hypoplasia.

fields.Text: Lateral hindfoot impingement is driven by {{c1::hindfoot valgus from adult acquired flatfoot}}, not primarily repetitive sinus tarsi trauma.
fields.Extra: Q4. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: T1 hypointense signal in lateral talus and calcaneus represents talocalcaneal impingement from adult acquired flatfoot deformity, where hindfoot valgus causes abnormal bone contact and marrow edema.

fields.Text: A Hill-Sachs defect implies prior {{c1::anterior shoulder dislocation}}.
fields.Extra: Q7. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. The frontal shoulder radiograph shows deformity at the posterior lateral aspect of the humeral head (green arrows) consistent with a Hill-Sachs impaction fracture.

fields.Text: Dose calibrator linearity is tested {{c1::quarterly}}, while accuracy is tested {{c2::annually}}.
fields.Extra: Q16. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Dose calibrator QC includes four essential tests with specific frequencies: daily constancy testing ensures day-to-day reliability, quarterly linearity testing verifies accurate response across all activity ranges, annual accuracy testing confirms measurement precision against traceable standards, and geometry testing at installation/repair ensures consistent readings across different vial and syringe sizes. This tiered approach balances patient safety with practical workflow demands.

fields.Text: In an early post-renal-transplant patient, tracer activity in the surgical drain indicates {{c1::urinary/anastomotic leak}}.
fields.Extra: Q20. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. This is a Tc99m-MAG3 renal scan.

fields.Text: A cyclops lesion after ACL reconstruction is localized anterior {{c1::arthrofibrosis}}, not a graft tear.
fields.Extra: Q26. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: A cyclops lesion (localized anterior arthrofibrosis) is a common complication of reconstruction of the anterior cruciate ligament caused by excessive scar tissue formation within the joint capsule. It consists of a rounded fibrous mass in the anterior intercondylar notch that is formed of central granulation tissued lined by synovium and surrounded by dense fibrous tissue. This entity can also be iatrogenic/post-traumatic, as seen in this case.

fields.Text: HASTE speeds T2 imaging by collecting {{c1::more data points per TR interval}}.
fields.Extra: Q28. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Half-Fourier-Acquired Single-shot Turbo spin Echo (HASTE) is a Turbo spin-echo technique that is used for sequential acquisition of high-resolution T2-weighted images.

fields.Text: The abnormal postmenopausal endometrial thickness threshold is {{c1::5 mm}}.
fields.Extra: Q29. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. The sagittal uterine ultrasound demonstrates a slightly heterogeneous-appearing endometrium with a thickness of 1.8cm (red arrow), which is abnormally thickened.

fields.Text: Osteochondromas are most often {{c1::low-grade/benign lesions}}, not high-grade malignancies.
fields.Extra: Q37. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The axial fat-saturated T2 and proton density MR images show a pedunculated bony excrescence with both cortical (red arrow) and medullary (yellow arrow) components, as well as a hyperintense cartilage cap (orange arrows).

fields.Text: A volar tendon sheath mass in a finger with homogeneous enhancement suggests {{c1::giant cell tumor of the tendon sheath}}.
fields.Extra: Q38. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: T1 iso/hypointense ovoid mass involving volar tendon sheath with homogeneous enhancement suggests localized tenosynovial giant cell tumor, typically presenting as painless soft tissue mass in fingers.

fields.Text: Reticular T2 hyperintense bands in cirrhosis represent {{c1::fibrosis}}, not active inflammation.
fields.Extra: Q39. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Reticular T2 hyperintense signal in cirrhotic liver represents fibrotic septa and bridges with high water content that surround regenerative nodules - this net-like pattern of T2 hyperintense fibrosis is a key imaging feature of advanced cirrhosis.

fields.Text: A type 2 endoleak comes from {{c1::retrograde collateral branch-vessel flow}} into the aneurysm sac.
fields.Extra: Q44. Incorrect; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Type 2 endoleaks result from retrograde collateral flow into the aneurysm sac. Patent branch vessels (primarily IMA and lumbar arteries) remain unsealed after EVAR and can persistently flow into the excluded aneurysm sac.

fields.Text: A cortically based lucent lesion with a thin sclerotic rim is classic for {{c1::non-ossifying fibroma}}.
fields.Extra: Q46. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. The imaging characteristics of this lesion include a cortically-based lucent centered lesion with a thin, sclerotic rim.

fields.Text: Prominent LV trabeculation with deep intertrabecular recesses is {{c1::left ventricular non-compaction}}.
fields.Extra: Q48. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. This is an abnormal arrest in the process of myocardial compaction leading to the development of deep intertrabecular recesses and prominent ventricular trabeculation.

fields.Text: To increase radiographic subject contrast, {{c1::lower kVp}}.
fields.Extra: Q50. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Lowering kVp improves subject contrast as differential attenuation between tissues is greater at lower energies.
